reviews.review_description:
This game is really fun!!! If it feels too difficult, you should switch the spaceship controls and targeting to the VR headset in the settings instead of using the joystick—it makes aiming super easy, and enemies practically have no chance of shaking you off in close combat!

I really hope more content will be added to the game (more missions, more weapons, and maybe even another spaceship). So far, though, it’s exactly what I was hoping for! Very well done—please keep it up!!!

reviews.review_title: You cant refund it so dont even buy
reviews.review_description: I thought it LOOKED good in the preview but once I bought it, it was Terrible I tried to refund it but I couldn't so dont even try to refund it if you buy it

Well looked like a great game, looks good sounds good, I liked it a lot. Mission on the comms array. Taking a piss guys - the difficulty level goes insane.

Starting to think that there is nothing after that level as I cannot see anyone ever beating it.

Someone said that the first mission take you over the refund time. Clever scam.

Developers come on - this has so much potential.

developer_response.body:
'Someone said that the first mission take you over the refund time.' - That is just not true. The first mission only takes 5 minutes.

As for the difficulty in the mission Comms Array, Be sure to take out the missile turrets on the asteroids first, that should help you!

reviews.review_description: I got tired of waiting for remnant protocol so I got this to fulfill my space shooter needs. Like some of the others have said, it’s short but replay-able in my opinion. They really should expand on it more. Could be so much more than a short space campaign.
I Just bought the game 1 hour ago and I finished already there is no coop or online no single player having fun by yourself is just few short mission and that's it !!! End of game to be continued

I got this game way back in 2015 on the Gear VR and loved it. After the device was made obsolete I never went back to VR until getting the Quest 3s. Nostalgia got the better of me and had to get it. Unfortunately it wasn't free because I had it already but what are you going to do? $15 is probably a bit steep but with a coupon I got it for $10. And it's worth it. I've noticed better graphics but generally it's the same game. I never got to finish the missions that are available so it will be nice to get them done. The controls work well, gaze to fly is the best option for flight control. It could probably have done with a slightly wider range of weaponry but it's perfectly fine as is. A great space shooter overall and you really feel that you're in the cockpit of a space fighter.  I have heard that no new promised missions ever arrived and it ends with "to be continued" but I will wait to see when I get to that point. I do recommend it, particularly if you can get it at a discounted price. It was the best game on the Gear VR and it still holds up very well today, despite being around almost 10 years. I'd love to see the developers, if they're still doing vr games to revisit this and update it with new missions.

I don't see why people are leaving negative reviews. The missions are all unique, and highly replayable. The fact that people are only saying the number of weapons 3 guns. 2 missiles, but not their purpose is quite insulting to the game.

You get:
A machine gun - good at most ranges, good fire rate, moderate damage.
A shotgun - good at close and mid range, slow fire rate, greater damage.
A sniper style weapon - I don't own it, so I cannot speak for it, but considering the way the other weapons work, if I had to hazard a guess, I would say it's amazing at long range, but poor at other ranges, great for singling out targets one by one.
Basic missiles - good for one target at a time, locks fast. Fire and tracks fast.
Swarm missiles - they are slower but leave an explosion for taking swarms out.

These aren't any of the actual weapon names. But you can get a basic idea.

This game is short, yes, but it doesn't need to be too long when you have one of every kind of mission to play. Would I like to see more on the future? Yes. But for 15 bucks and on the Quest 2, I can't ask for more.

This game is like VR Star Fox, except instead of a space furry you play as yourself. So probably a space furry.

Cant say enough about it really. Once you learn to fly and use your rockets, your all set. Bummer there isn't more but according to the developer, he's making a new game that, according to him will be better in every way!

Nope, it could have been good, but you get 11 missions and then a never to come "to be continued." BS. 3 weapons, 2 missiles. Boring missions, no real immersion. A poor effort and an insult to space combat with what is possible in VR at their disposal.

The potential for this game is endless. Reminds me of a pc game call Freespace 1 & 2. Those who know know!! EndSpace could be this years best seller but it need that complete freespace 2 update. Dog fights And ￼ multiplayer Missions would take this on a whole new level. Yes there are levels to this!￼ team matches running the gauntlet ‘’those who know knows’’  The bombs Guided missiles nukes was all that and a bag of chips. The support and rearming ship. The ability to call in a Beta ￼ squad and give Beta team directives like disable capital ships ￼made the game more like a realistic experience in space. Hey Meta have a look at Freespace 2 game play. Get those guys back and Finnish the job please. Also when this works.please don’t forget  me thank you.
Terrible game. And for some strange reason you can’t even get a refund ..

It isn’t finished, randomly you hit the end mission and it isn’t even an end. Just says to be continued. 

Could be a great game, but they need to update the engine (graphics are very rudimentary and could be done much better). 

Ultimately it is a decent buy if you can get it on a massive discount.

Could this be turned into a multiplayer game.
If possible please do. Would pay more as this game has a lot of good points

This game is unbelievably bad. I'm only in the tutorial and this is already bad. The graphics are horrible it looks like it should be for a PS1. The tutorial map is so bland it's just a few rocks and a planet. If you got stick drift don't buy this because you move the ship with the joystick. Combat is okay the enemies move as soon I get near them which is really annoying. Trying to turn the ship is a nightmare I feel so slow when I try to turn it.
I feel ripped off. The game promises so much and just as you think the game is getting interesting it ends. Completed all by one mission first time and then get a "To Be Continued".
Wouldn’t buy it if I could have played it first.  Very basic

The game has content like a demo. Just when you get started it ends. There is a sign "Coming soon" but it is not true. It is just a showcase of what a great game could it be in the price of an actual game. Sorry, but not worth it. In that money you can get huge flying sim like Warplanes WWI - you can't get wrong with that one, the amout of content is mindblowing - that is what Endspace should be!

Decent game but gets too hard and very unfair.
Out numbered and outgunned early in
The shop is pathetic, two separate weapon menus but nothing for: armor, speed & damage 
up to my weapons?
I asked for a refund after 2 minutes of play on the day of purchase and the refund was impossible.  No logical reason… I consider it a scam

reviews.review_title: In need of an update or 12
reviews.review_description: Optimised for Quest 2 should tell you everything you need to know. Really not worth it in 2026.

I nearly didn’t buy this after reading some of the reviews but so glad I did. This is the kind of game I bought the quest for. Great fun blasting enemies. Very immersive and absorbing and didn’t make me nauseous. The control system seems to work well using the joysticks on the controller. In terms of value I think it’s great compared to the Vader game which only lasted about 40 mins. I’m on my fourth mission here and it looks like there’s plenty more. Great job from the devs and I look forward to their next offering.

Update: Not long after writing this i got stuck on a level and just can’t seem to get passed it. I’ve upgraded my kit as much as possible but still can’t progress which is a bit frustrating. I still play it because the gameplay is great but it would be better if there was a way to either dial down the difficulty or get more weapons to improve my chances of progress. Also it would be amazing if the combat elements of the game were delivered within a more involved strategic dimension.

reviews.review_title: This could be a great game, but its not
reviews.review_description:
The game have awesome control, and kinda fun, BUT it didnt have another spaceships, very easy enemy, lack of weapon variances, NO PROMISING STORY, no video animation.. its just seems unfinish and left by the developers.
It was great at first, but after the 2nd mission it feels empty

Nothing is good but there is some effort about the different way you can control the starship. 
No immersive feeling.
No fun to shoot. 
Even make that comment is a lose of time.

I absolutely love this game. It’s the one game that I’ve found that lets you fly jets/ships with military-grade weapons, not to mention the virtual controller that lets you feel like a fighter pilot too. I’m not even halfway done with this game, and the missions keep getting better, but I feel like this seriously needs a graphics update, and a multiplayer mode. If it were possible to create servers to support maybe even 5v5 Starcraft fights, plus a destroyer or two, would definitely have a huge impact. Sincerely, a satisfied customer.
They could do alot to make this better but for what it is already it's worth it when it's on sale.

reviews.review_title: It wasn’t the game I was looking for
reviews.review_description:
I was trying to get a good game and this looked like it so I bought it you can’t even enter the planets all you do is shoot not worth £11 it’s worth £2 DONT BUY only buy if you want to shoot floating rocks advertising is fake it said racing there’s no racing terrible game buy
 if you like wasting money also the trailer is nothing like the game most of the trailer is looks like no mans sky (I recommend no mans sky its a good space game) massive spaceships are only in the trailer I only played for 2 minutes because it was so boring it doesn’t tell you how to play it doesn’t even tell you how to get into the missions the Ai move around every second it doesn’t get harder every mission it does tell you how to shoot so I’m sitting there pressing all the buttons and bye then the enemy’s have already killed me the people saying it’s a good game are bots if the bots where Gone this game would have zero stars I wish I could give it zero stars it should give you money to play

When I first got the headset. I bought five games. I kept coming back to this one, and I finished it, and then replayed some of the missions. I haven't finished any of the other ones yet. It's relatively straightforward but very fun.
This is without a doubt one of the best games I've played in fact it's excellent
A bit difficult to get used too but amazing experience!!!

I love this game, and am ready for the next story expansion.  Not only is it well paced fun it doesn't even get me sick.  The extra things i would like to see added are more upgrades for the ship; and a multi-player mode would also be nice.  I definitely recommend  this game to anyone with a vr system

Decent graphical upgrade for Q2, controls well, however it takes more than that to make a game fun.  

If you want a game with AI straight out of the 80s and will make you fall asleep while playing a mission get this one on sale. Zzzzzzzz.

reviews.review_title: Wing Commander fans...this is a MUST
reviews.review_description: Back in the 90s, A game called Wing Commander became my absolute favorite game to play...The game was loved by millions, spawned into 5 different games and lasted over a decade. It was my favorite game until now. To the developers...PLEASE TELL ME you have serious plans to continue this story and make more games based on it. If so you have a life long customer.

Great game, can't wait for more to be added. I like the controls as they are, intuitive if you've played flight games with a keyboard. This is the game you buy a VR system for, this is the experience of space combat

Nice graphics, the environment feels huge, games are challenging but not impossible. Controlling the ship is hard to get used to, but that may be just my poor hand- eye skills. Overall  a great experience.

Definitely enjoyable but it seems to be an introduction to a longer campaign that just doesn't exist.  Leaves it off at a bit of a cliffhanger at the end and I doubt they'll be any updates to this version at this point.

Fun for what it is, but not a ton of replayability after you finish the main campaign.  I'd certainly recommend it if it's on sale.

It’s not 3D, the “space” part of space is more of a spherical picture. It gave me motion sickness after only 5 minutes of playing.  I haven’t finished the story but from the other reviews it is fairly short.  After reading only half of the instructions on how to fly, they disappeared leaving me to figure it out by myself.  If the developers put lots more time into this game then it could definitely be better.  Otherwise it’s just a waste of $15.

I've always hated joystick based games It never feels like natural flight... The recent updates for the Oculus Quest 2 Make this flying experience feel like I'm actually in the ship. Being able to free look even over the edge of my cockpit for an enemy is just way too cool. After one or two dog fights you don't even notice the pitching yaw controls and the movement becomes natural. This review only given after a few missions.

Game like this is what VR was intended for I loved this game it’s pace is fast and the learning curve is just right. The controls can be a little tricky to get right but once you have your preference dialed in it’s a dream to fly.

The missions are intense and  the  difficulty curve is good . The Seek and destroy and defend missions both add too the challenge.  I would like to see a squad cooperative mode and a multiplayer player dog fight option. but that would just be a little icing on an already tasty cake. 

Has a casual gamer like myself this is perfect you can spend just a little time immersed in the game or go for a full evening of blasting 

Not sure I get the weapon upgrade section I didn’t notice any change with the various missile and weapons upgrades but to be honest It did not take away any enjoyment of the experience .

Sign up contractor take the fight controls and join the battle

If you haven't experienced VR space dog fighting yet and you don't have access to Starwars Squadrons, then this is literally the only best thing if you're on an Oculus Quest 1/2.

 I read through a lot of reviews here and quickly discovered you can hook up an Xbox controller and set the control settings exactly how you want. That's the first thing that sold me. In fact, based on some reviews and some youtube dialogue about the default controls with the standard Quest controllers that's what was setting me off to buy the game. I didn't like either the idea to fly by gaze or shoot by gaze. Both turned me off to the game. Therefore, most people blaze through on this easy controller setting and then complain that it's too short. Nevertheless, depending how you set you controller on a gamepad like my xbox controller you can experience more difficulty. I can confirm that is the case. The first five missions are simple, but after a certain point you need to start thinking about how to fly better and experiment with the few upgrades the game offers. As a side note, I wouldn't mind hooking up a Hotas on this game for even more immersion. Anyhow, even with the controller is better for gameplay, you fly your ship the standard way. The only thing gaze does is lock on missiles to the target. Lol, when I was in the tutorial section I was annoyed by it and tried looking everywhere to see if I can turn it off. Didn't realize at the time it's a gameplay mechanic that feels good to use so now I like it. 

This game is $15 and it's well worth that to experience a more immersive arcade style space combat VR game. If this was in the Xbox store it would have been with games with gold's already and selling for $1.99. The fact that it would be your first VR space fighting game though makes it much more than ever $15. Although, when I get a PC to run Starwars Squadrons I doubt I'll ever retur Nevertheless that's months away. And right now
Never really been interested in gaming I'm 57 yes 57 👍so this oculas 2 game blows my mind get it what an experience 😃

reviews.review_title: Don't even think about buying this game
reviews.review_description: Please don't waste your time or your money Trust me
Please bring another end space out I beg, this game was so good
Who doesnt love shooting fighter ships in a space world.... Once I got over my initial motion dizziness and I got the hang of the controls I found this game immersive and enjoyable to play.

reviews.review_title: A great game by a single developer
reviews.review_description:
I usually don't write reviews, but I've read some of the bad reviews about this game, and I understood from the replies that this is a one man show project, so I decided to give it a try anyways.

I was blown by the amount of effort this single developer put into this game. Yes, you can complain about the lack of deep story, and you could wish for better graphics (although I think it's great).

As a community we need to support talented developers who put their neck out, and provide us with the best experience they can.

The gameplay is smooth, there are many options to fly the ship and shoot, and it's a lot of fun to play. Not to mention the breath taking environments. It also does not make me feel dizzy which is a miracle in itself.

Thank you for taking the time to build such an awesome space shooter experience. Reminds me of wing commander 😀

This is a great game, great graphics and game play with some brilliant missions. 

It does however need more upgrades other than the missiles and guns. Maybe shields, new ships extra guns or missile slots. 

When the sequel comes out or the add on I’ll 100% get it because this was well worth the money.

This game is a lot of fun and the controls are easy to adhere to. Free look is great, learn to look correctly to steer and turn your right hand to hone in on enemies. This is YOU inside a arcade space shooter and it legit feels like I’ve warped into an arcade space shooter, myself.  Sit down, relax and BLAST the enemy ships!!! I really appreciate the DEVS work here but my only hope is that they add more atmosphere with the nebula/planets/stars. More movement....etc. I would love to see an active star in action with flares and bursts while flying in space above it.
Basic

Once I got the hang of the look-to-steer control scheme, this game just clicked. I was having so much fun swooping around tracking enemies and blowing stuff up. Kudos to the developer, I totally recommend this game to anyone that likes arcade style space shooters.
good game but 1) too short 2) ai is kind of dump
Not a bad game could be a bit .more fast paced .also a few more better ships.
It’s really dizzy
This game is very fun yet simple as to move or shoot you do not make any interactions with the ship like moving the ship is just move joystick up or down

I am new to the VR world and this was the first game I purchased. Amazing! I am totally addicted! The graphics are spot on, challenging but so far manageable. I had heard some folks say this game is good but doesn't have much more content than a demo, I'm about to start mission #4 so I was pleasantly surprised. Well worth the money

The irony is how quickly the game comes to an end with huge space left on the mission wheel "To Be Continued."

I was excited to see a flight simulator style shooting game available. The trailer looked epic! I was even amazed during my introduction and game play experience with the graphics. 

But about 10min in my hands and arms were hurting with requiring to keep the controllers positioned correctly in order for the cannons to aim. (This was the least issue I had). 

During the larger scale battles while needing to blow up space stations. The scale ratios were extremely poor managed. Your spacecraft that should be able to fly through the enemy space station rings and other spaces would come to a sudden stop (or shall I call it "End Space"). Including with my issue of the scale ratio is the explosion of the enemies crafts and stations that are very cartooni and cheesy like some early 90s arcade game graphic show of blowing up a craft in space. This was very disappointing for 2020 technology. 

The biggest issue I had was the sudden end of the game. Like 11 missions? ( 2 hours top of play) Missions that lasted 4min-10min. With your weapon payload having only 2 missle options, and 3 cannon types to select from.  I would compare this game to going to prom when you spend your money on everything and getting hyped up in the beginning only for your date to give you a peck on the cheek at the end. 

Don't get me wrong. I love the concept! Even after playing... I respect the development team. This seemed more for a Commercial game where a customer doesn't have 2 hours to stay and play? But currently, how everything sits and is. It was not worth $14.99.  ($5.99) I would not been upset and feel taken advantage of. So I am hoping for my investment of $15. That the aspect ratio allowing to be more fluent in maneuvering around enemy space stations and more importantly expending the weapons and missions available will be available. I am looking forward for that time. Keep up the hard work! I believe you are on the right track in an entirely new gaming experience and keystone game.



P.S. what's with the HQ teleporting? Is that another work in progress to explore your homebase and be more interactive? Is there plans to be enteractive lobby with other players to que up for multiplyer missions?  Maybe even full scale galaxtic armada (PvP) cruisers, destroyers, warships, etc.

Still pretty new to VR, but of the handful of games I bought,  this one is my second favorite so far (I like space based shooters).  I did have a little issue with motion sickness at first, but after a while (and some of the tips I read), it is nearly non-existent now. I'll just list my biggest pro and con I have with it. 

Pro:
Fairly easy to play. Not too many controls to memorize. I find the joysticks a little too sensitive, but I have not adjusted any of the settings yet. 

Con:
It seems like the enemy ships are always behind you no matter how fast you turn around, and you are constantly just turning instead of engaging in fights.

I've played a few hrs or so, great visuals and impressive as created by a just 1 or 2 people I heard. Cheap price so worth trying out if you like Sci fi space flying games. 

However I feel frustration flying the ship and take hits I could have avoided due to the controls. Have 
tried gaze to fly, gaze to aim, invert sticks, lefthand flight but none of them feel right as the underlying movement of the ship feels clunky for me at least. 

If there were the ability to change the controller setting on VR controller this game would be a better experience . Currently has the boost button on Y or one of the facpad buttons so you can't boost and yaw same time as have to move thumb from the control stick. I want to boost and move tme ship where I want same time, not line it up first then boost letting the AI get easy shots off. 

Good control set if ever added would be Left stick turn ship and increase/decrease throttle, right stick up down and yaw, brake/boost on shoulder trigger button L/R respectively, guns on one side trigger missiles on the other, face buttons to change weapons. Can do virtually everything with a setup like that with thumb moving only to quickly change weapon. Really unsure why this wasn't covered off, ruins tne immersion for me. 

I could tether an wireless xbox controller and get this, but then might as well just play the xbox in reality ;)

Hope VR controller config support gets added, 5 star game with that

developer_response.body: I don't have a way to add configuring VR controllers. However if you have a Bluetooth Xbox controller, or any Bluetooth gamepad for that matter you can connect it to your Quest and use that for playing instead of the Touch controllers and that would allow you to customize the controllers.

reviews.review_description: This game is a good game its not what i expected.  But my expectations might have been. Little to high for any dame. I would buy this one again if I needed to its a good fun game and the morins sick esss wasn't there at all for me.
It sucks because I can't play it 😭😭😭😭
Laid
I’m not happy with this game because there is nothing but 3 options. One it’s to learn how to train to fly, 2 how to shoot and the last one is to shoot something. But that’s it for $15!
